message.content:
Map the full structure of /home/mega/OMEGA_WORKSPACE/CANON/OmegA-Architecture. I need to understand:
1. What framework/stack is it (Next.js, Vite, etc.)
2. What's in package.json (dependencies, scripts)
3. What API routes or backend exist
4. What environment variables are referenced (.env.example, .env.local, any config files)
5. What components already exist (list all files under src/components or similar)
6. What hooks exist (list all files under src/hooks or similar)
7. Is there any existing chat/AI integration code
8. What's the Vercel project name / vercel.json config
9. What's in any existing .env or env files (not VAULT, just the repo)
10. Full directory tree (top 3 levels)

Be thorough — list actual file paths.

1	import path from "node:path";
2	import type { NextConfig } from "next";
3	
4	// Security headers applied to every response (#16, #17, #30)
5	const SECURITY_HEADERS = [
6	  // Prevent clickjacking
7	  { key: "X-Frame-Options", value: "DENY" },
8	  // Stop MIME sniffing
9	  { key: "X-Content-Type-Options", value: "nosniff" },
10	  // Referrer policy — don't leak URLs to third parties
11	  { key: "Referrer-Policy", value: "strict-origin-when-cross-origin" },
12	  // Permissions policy — deny unnecessary browser APIs
13	  {
14	    key: "Permissions-Policy",
15	    value: "camera=(), microphone=(), geolocation=(), payment=()",
16	  },
17	  // Basic XSS protection for older browsers
18	  { key: "X-XSS-Protection", value: "1; mode=block" },
19	  // Strict Transport Security (HTTPS only)
20	  {
21	    key: "Strict-Transport-Security",
22	    value: "max-age=63072000; includeSubDomains; preload",
23	  },
24	  // Content Security Policy
25	  // Adjust 'connect-src' if you add new external API domains
26	  {
27	    key: "Content-Security-Policy",
28	    value: [
29	      "default-src 'self'",
30	      "script-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline' 'unsafe-eval'", // Next.js requires unsafe-eval in dev
31	      "style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'",
32	      "img-src 'self' data: blob:",
33	      "font-src 'self'",
34	      "connect-src 'self' https://ai-gateway.vercel.sh https://api.x.ai https://generativelanguage.googleapis.com https://api.openai.com",
35	      "frame-ancestors 'none'",
36	    ].join("; "),
37	  },
38	];
39	
40	const nextConfig: NextConfig = {
41	  turbopack: {
42	    // Keep the workspace root pinned to the actual Next app instead of the wider home directory.
43	    root: path.resolve(__dirname),
44	  },
45	
46	  async headers() {
47	    return [
48	      {
49	        // Apply security headers to all routes
50	        source: "/(.*)",
51	        headers: SECURITY_HEADERS,
52	      },
53	      {
54	        // CORS for API routes — restrict to same origin in production (#16)
55	        source: "/api/(.*)",
56	        headers: [
57	          { key: "Access-Control-Allow-Origin", value: process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL ?? "*" },
58	          { key: "Access-Control-Allow-Methods", value: "GET, POST, OPTIONS" },
59	          { key: "Access-Control-Allow-Headers", value: "Content-Type, x-omega-operator-token" },
60	        ],
61	      },
62	    ];
63	  },
64	};
65	
66	export default nextConfig;
67

1	This is the public-facing Next.js runtime for OmegA.
2	
3	The web runtime now supports two server-enforced interaction postures:
4	
5	- `public`: curated public corpus only, public-safe disclosure boundary, firm public-facing stance
6	- `operator`: privileged sovereign posture for the operator, enabled only with `OMEGA_OPERATOR_TOKEN`
7	
8	The mode split is resolved on the server before retrieval and model invocation. It is not prompt-only.
9	
10	## Getting Started
11	
12	First, run the development server:
13	
14	```bash
15	npm run dev
16	# or
17	yarn dev
18	# or
19	pnpm dev
20	# or
21	bun dev
22	```
23	
24	Open [http://localhost:3000](http://localhost:3000) with your browser to see the result.
25	
26	You can start editing the page by modifying `app/page.tsx`. The page auto-updates as you edit the file.
27	
28	This project uses [`next/font`](https://nextjs.org/docs/app/building-your-application/optimizing/fonts) to automatically optimize and load [Geist](https://vercel.com/font), a new font family for Vercel.
29	
30	## Environment Variables
31	
32	Core runtime:
33	
34	- `DATABASE_URL`
35	- `VERCEL_AI_GATEWAY_KEY`
36	- `OMEGA_UI_PASSWORD` (basic gate for the web app)
37	- `OMEGA_OPERATOR_TOKEN` (separate secret for operator-mode elevation)
38	
39	LiveKit (optional):
40	
41	- `NEXT_PUBLIC_LIVEKIT_ENABLED` (`true` to enable LiveKitGate)
42	- `NEXT_PUBLIC_LIVEKIT_URL` (LiveKit server URL)
43	- `NEXT_PUBLIC_LIVEKIT_TOKEN_ENDPOINT` (optional, defaults to `/api/livekit/token`)
44	- `NEXT_PUBLIC_LIVEKIT_STATUS_BADGE` (`true` to show the UI status badge)
45	- `LIVEKIT_API_KEY` (server-side)
46	- `LIVEKIT_API_SECRET` (server-side)
47	- `LIVEKIT_ROOM` (optional, default room name for tokens)
48	
49	## Learn More
50	
51	To learn more about Next.js, take a look at the following resources:
52	
53	- [Next.js Documentation](https://nextjs.org/docs) - learn about Next.js features and API.
54	- [Learn Next.js](https://nextjs.org/learn) - an interactive Next.js tutorial.
55	
56	You can check out [the Next.js GitHub repository](https://github.com/vercel/next.js) - your feedback and contributions are welcome!
57	
58	## Deploy on Vercel
59	
60	The easiest way to deploy your Next.js app is to use the [Vercel Platform](https://vercel.com/new?utm_medium=default-template&filter=next.js&utm_source=create-next-app&utm_campaign=create-next-app-readme) from the creators of Next.js.
61	
62	Check out our [Next.js deployment documentation](https://nextjs.org/docs/app/building-your-application/deploying) for more details.
63	
64	## Verification
65	
66	Run the canonical local checks:
67	
68	```bash
69	npm run lint
70	npm run build
71	```
72	
73	Run the deployed smoke checks:
74	
75	```bash
76	node web/scripts/omega-smoke.mjs --base https://your-deployment.vercel.app
77	node web/scripts/public-boundary-smoke.mjs --base https://your-deployment.vercel.app
78	```
79	

<system-reminder>

But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.

*  #4  Over-scripted / under-validated input — schema validation
 *  #5  Wordy / oversized input — character caps
 *  #7  Graceful error handling — never crash a stream silently
 *  #13 Context loss / long-chat drift — session history window cap
 *  #15 Edge cases & adversarial inputs — expanded injection patterns
 *  #16 Prompt injection & jailbreaks — deeper pattern set + rate gate
 *  #17 Data privacy — error sanitisation, no raw internals to client
 *  #21 Latency & scalability — lightweight in-memory rate limiter
 *  #22 Cost-driven collapse — token budget enforcement
 *  #23 Observability — structured request logging
 *  #24 Rushed launches — request schema validation at boundary
 *
 * Round 2 (voice/transport/infra):
 *  V1  Client/UX — mic permission error surface, TTS length cap, markdown strip
 *  V2  Transport — correlation IDs for full pipeline tracing
 *  V3  ASR — audio file validation (size, MIME type)
 *  V4  NLU/LLM — dialog loop detection
 *  V5  Tools/APIs — circuit breaker per provider, exponential backoff
 *  V6  TTS — markdown stripping before synthesis
 *  V7  Infrastructure — health check utilities, graceful degradation mode
 *  V8  Observability — correlation ID threading
 *  V9  Security — STT/TTS auth enforcement (key protection)
 *  #29 Mobile / accessibility — documented here, enforced in components
 */

  MAX_HISTORY_CHARS: 20_000,   // total characters across all history turns
  MAX_SESSION_ID_LEN: 64,
} as const;

content.text:
Perfect! Now I have enough information. Let me create a comprehensive report.

## OmegA-Architecture Web Stack Mapping

### 1. **Framework & Stack**

**Next.js 16.2.1** with React 19.2.4

- Server: Node.js runtime
- Type system: TypeScript 5
- Build tool: Next.js Turbopack
- Frontend framework: React 19
- UI patterns: Server Components + Client Components

### 2. **Package Dependencies**

**Web Dependencies** (`/home/mega/OMEGA_WORKSPACE/CANON/OmegA-Architecture/web/package.json`):

```json
{
  "@ai-sdk/openai": "^3.0.48",
  "@anthropic-ai/sdk": "^0.80.0",
  "@google/genai": "^1.46.0",
  "@livekit/components-react": "^2.9.20",
  "@neondatabase/serverless": "^1.0.2",
  "@types/three": "^0.183.1",
  "@vercel/speed-insights": "^2.0.0",
  "ai": "^6.0.137",
  "livekit-server-sdk": "^2.7.1",
  "livekit-client": "^2.18.0",
  "next": "16.2.1",
  "react": "19.2.4",
  "react-dom": "19.2.4",
  "react-markdown": "^10.1.0",
  "remark-gfm": "^4.0.1",
  "three": "^0.183.2"
}
```

**Dev Dependencies**: TypeScript, ESLint, @types/node, @types/react, @types/react-dom

### 3. **API Routes & Backend**

**Location**: `/home/mega/OMEGA_WORKSPACE/CANON/OmegA-Architecture/web/src/app/api/`

| Route | Purpose |
|-------|---------|
| `/api/chat` | Streaming chat with multi-provider fallback ( → OpenAI → Grok via Vercel Gateway) |
| `/api/ask` | Non-streaming text generation for voice synthesis |
| `/api/research` | External knowledge retrieval endpoint |
| `/api/refine` | Query refinement and disambiguation |
| `/api/health` | Liveness/readiness probe |
| `/api/providers` | Provider health snapshot + routing metadata |
| `/api/runtime-status` | OmegA subsystem status |
| `/api/knowledge` | Knowledge graph / memory indexing |
| `/api/signals` | Teleodynamic signal ingestion |
| `/api/sessions` | Session lifecycle management (CRUD) |
| `/api/sessions/[id]` | Get session by ID |
| `/api/stt` | Speech-to-text (audio → text) |
| `/api/tts` | Text-to-speech synthesis |
| `/api/synthesize` | Non-streaming synthesis directive |
| `/api/livekit/token` | LiveKit room token generation |
| `/api/operator/session` | Operator-mode elevation & session management |

---

## Key Takeaways

1. **Framework**: Modern Next.js 16 with React 19, deployed via Vercel
2. **AI Stack**: Multi-provider LLM fallback ( → OpenAI → Grok via Vercel AI Gateway)
3. **Database**: Neon serverless PostgreSQL for memory/phylactery persistence
4. **Architecture**: Full OmegA four-layer system embedded in the web runtime (AEGIS, AEON, ADCCL, MYELIN)
5. **Voice**: LiveKit + native Web Speech API + ElevenLabs TTS
6. **Security**: Server-enforced mode split (public vs operator), comprehensive hardening layer, CSP headers
7. **Interaction Modes**: Two postures enforced server-side before retrieval — `public` (curated corpus) and `operator` (privileged)
8. **Total Source Files**: 55 TypeScript/TSX files (~1,691 LOC in components alone)
